study_title: Transcriptional responses in honey bee larvae infected with chalkbrood fungus
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Apis mellifera: midgut in experiment group  of T/T genotype 3
design: the mRNAs were enriched using the oligo(dT) magnetic beads, fragmented into short pieces (about 300 nt) by NEBNext UltraTM RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (NEB, USA), and then cDNAs were synthesized by random hexamer primer and M-MuLV Reverse Transcriptase(RNase H-). Finally, sequencing adaptors were ligated to the cDNA fragments by PCR amplication.
sample_description: the midguts of T/T larvae from treatment group

exp_title: Midgut polyA RNA of Apis mellifera Forager (Colony 1)
design: Libraries were made with the NEBNext Illumina RNA-Seq library kit according to the manufacturer’s instructions. Sequencing was performed on the HiSeq 2000 (100 bp paired end).
sample_title: Midgut polyA RNA of Apis mellifera (Forager)
sample_description: Midgut of the Forager caste of Apis mellifera. Tissues were stored at -80C. RNA was extracted using Trizol protocl. Tissues homogenized in Trizol within 5 minutes of dissection.
center: UC Davis Department of Entomology and Nematology
biomaterial_provider: Harry H. Laidlaw Jr. Honey Bee Research Facility 1 Bee Biology Rd, Davis, CA 95616
